technical.description: Identification of IPSAS compliance issues, correct treatment, quantification of financial impact.

embedded_issues.title: Valencia office lease expensed instead of capitalised under IPSAS 43
embedded_issues.expected: Recognise as right-of-use asset and lease liability; calculate ROU asset $0.6m+, lease liability c. $0.6m, restate operating expense down by $220k.

embedded_issues.title: UNEP $6.6m three-year contract recognised in full in 20X5
embedded_issues.expected: Should be $550k revenue + $6.05m deferred revenue. Adjust revenue down by $6.05m and surplus by the same; recognise deferred revenue liability.

embedded_issues.title: No ECL provision against $4.2m of receivables over 12 months overdue
embedded_issues.expected: ECL provision required, especially for the $1.8m flagged uncollectible. Recognise impairment charge; estimate provision in the range $1.8m to $4.2m depending on assumptions.

embedded_issues.title: Research-phase staff costs ($1.1m of $3.2m UniCloud) capitalised in error
embedded_issues.expected: Research costs cannot be capitalised under IPSAS 31. Reverse $1.1m capitalisation, expense to staff costs; reduce intangibles.

embedded_issues.title: GBP imprest translated at opening rate (1.22) not closing rate (1.27)
embedded_issues.expected: Restate at closing rate; recognise $7.4k unrealised translation gain; flag as control gap even though immaterial.

embedded_issues.title: Discount rate change (3.10% → 4.20%) and $3.4m impact not disclosed
embedded_issues.expected: Add narrative disclosure of assumption change, rationale, and quantified impact; sensitivity analysis.

embedded_issues.title: Concentric Governance Advisory engagement (HR spouse) not disclosed
embedded_issues.expected: Disclose related-party relationship, contract value $45k, governance/procurement implications even though deliverables genuine.

investigation_quality.description: How systematically and effectively the candidate used the IDSC Financial Analysis System.
indicators: Walks through balance sheet and income statement systematically rather than asking generic 'find me errors' prompts
Probes accounting policies and methodology, not just balances
Asks targeted follow-up questions when answers reveal something off (e.g., 'is X disclosed in the notes?')
Quantifies impact rather than just identifying issues qualitatively
Cross-references between the exhibit and the system
Recognises that the system is a passive oracle and frames questions accordingly

professional_skills.description: Quality of the memo to the Chief of MS Division.
indicators: Memo is appropriately structured (issue / impact / recommendation)
Issues prioritised by financial materiality, not order of discovery
Recommendations are practical and time-bound (pre-audit submission)
Tone is professional, not lecturing — peer to senior management
Acknowledges judgmental areas (e.g., ECL estimate range) rather than presenting single-point answers as certain
Explicitly distinguishes 'must fix before submission' from 'should improve for next year'

technical.description: Identifying the four deliberate gaps in the AI cost analytics report.

embedded_issues.title: Shared infrastructure ($18.4m) allocated by revenue, not actual usage
embedded_issues.expected: Identify that small partners are over-charged on a per-unit basis. Propose usage-based allocation using available network management data (45/30/15/10 split).

embedded_issues.title: Sovereign AI cost understated by $4.1m due to cost-centre coding error
embedded_issues.expected: Identify that 8 reassigned FTEs are still charged to originating service lines. Recompute true Sovereign AI cost ($12.3m) and unit-cost benchmark (12% above commercial, not 32% below).

embedded_issues.title: Single $185k blended staff rate masks Geneva/Valencia $218k vs $112k split
embedded_issues.expected: Identify that Valencia-heavy services (Application Hosting) are over-costed and Geneva-heavy services (Cybersecurity) are under-costed. Propose location-specific rates.

embedded_issues.title: WHO 8% admin charge ($5.47m) sits unallocated, inflating service-line surplus
embedded_issues.expected: Identify that real cost recovery on each service line is lower than reported; propose allocation by staff cost.

investigation_quality.description: Sandbox investigation of underlying cost data.
indicators: Asks for actual usage data on shared infrastructure (the system has it)
Probes the Sovereign AI staffing model and discovers the cost-centre coding gap
Asks about staff costs by location
Identifies the WHO charge treatment by asking about overhead allocation
Recomputes unit costs rather than accepting reported numbers

judgment.description: Handling the Sovereign AI political dimension. This is the test of professional courage, stakeholder navigation, and decision under uncertainty.

rubric.13-15: Recognises the situation: Director has publicly cited an incorrect figure that the candidate now knows is wrong. Recommends a clear path that protects the Centre's credibility (correct the figure with the Management Committee BEFORE wider exposure; offer the Director language to reframe rather than recant; address the cost-centre coding root cause). Acknowledges the political reality without flinching from the professional obligation. Distinguishes options the candidate would advise vs decisions for the Director.
rubric.9-12: Recognises the issue but recommendation is partial — either too timid (suggests 'monitoring' or quietly fixing it next cycle) or too blunt (recommends public retraction without considering reputational mechanics).
rubric.5-8: Identifies the cost discrepancy but treats it as a pure accounting issue, missing the political dimension entirely. Recommends 'restate the numbers' without addressing how.
rubric.0-4: Endorses the analysis or fails to identify the discrepancy. Aligns with the Director's narrative.

professional_skills.description: Memo quality, practical recommendations, tone appropriate for senior management.
indicators: Structured around the four asks (a–d) the Chief of MS Division made
Distinguishes findings from recommendations
Tone is candid but supportive of the Chief — not lecturing, not deferential
Recommendations are sequenced (do this first, then this)
Acknowledges what cannot be fixed before the Management Committee meeting and proposes a defensible interim position
References stakeholder impact (partners, Director, Audit Committee)
